blurb: London/US voice-AI company. Faces repeated US litigation over alleged unconsented voice cloning — a 2024 voice-actor suit and a 2026 suit by journalists and voice actors in Illinois.

publications.title: Advancing AI voice security with ElevenLabs
publications.summary: The UK AI Security Institute announced research with ElevenLabs on whether people can distinguish AI from human voices and how that affects trust.

publications.title: ElevenLabs and UK Government partner on voice AI safety research
publications.summary: ElevenLabs announced a three-year UK Government partnership on voice-AI safety research, public-sector uses and talent collaboration.

publications.title: Guardrails 2.0: A redesigned control layer in ElevenAgents
publications.summary: ElevenLabs describes policy-violation controls and execution modes for guardrails in voice-agent deployments.

controversies.title: Amer et al v. Eleven Labs Inc. — BIPA proposed class action over alleged unconsented voiceprint training (N.D. Ill., 1:26-cv-05437)
controversies.court: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
controversies.summary: ElevenLabs is a named defendant in Amer et al v. Eleven Labs Inc., case no. 1:26-cv-05437, a proposed class action filed ~12 May 2026 in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Illinois. Seven plaintiffs — Illinois-resident broadcast journalists, podcasters, audio storytellers and voice actors (incl. Robin Amer; Carol Marin named among the wider group; Lindsay Dorcus, Victoria Nassif, Yohance Lacour, Alison Flowers) — allege the company harvested their voiceprints from the internet and fed them into AI training pipelines without the written notice, consent or retention policy that Illinois' Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA, 2008) requires. Plaintiffs' counsel is civil-rights firm Loevy + Loevy (Ross Kimbarovsky). The ElevenLabs suit is one of a coordinated wave of BIPA voice-AI class actions filed the same week against Adobe, Amazon, Apple, Google/Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, NVIDIA and Samsung. It is the second suit against ElevenLabs over voice use, following the earlier 2024 voice-actor misappropriation case Vacker v. ElevenLabs, Inc. (1:24-cv-00987) — which was filed in the District of Delaware, not Illinois (an earlier note in this feed imprecisely placed the prior suit; corrected here). All claims are alleged and at an early/pre-merits stage; no ruling reported.

corroboration.outlet: Law360 (case docket page — Amer et al v. Eleven Labs Inc., 1:26-cv-05437)
Bloomberg Law (2024 Vacker actors' suit, D. Del.)

notes:
Controversy attribution: named-party (ElevenLabs Inc. is the named defendant), same-entity (the text-to-speech voice-AI firm = seed elevenlabs-london). 2026-06-27 follow-up RESOLVED: traced to court docket. Case = Amer et al v. Eleven Labs Inc., no. 1:26-cv-05437, N.D. Ill., filed ~12 May 2026; counsel Loevy + Loevy; BIPA voiceprint claim; part of a same-week coordinated wave vs Adobe/Amazon/Apple/Google/Meta/Microsoft/NVIDIA/Samsung. Docket no., court, plaintiffs and counsel cross-confirmed across Law360 case page, Loevy + Loevy (plaintiffs' counsel), Capitol News Illinois, CBS Chicago and Sifted. Also corrected the prior-suit reference: the 2024 ElevenLabs voice-actor case is Vacker v. ElevenLabs, Inc. (1:24-cv-00987), filed in D. Del. (NOT Illinois). Severity held at medium: real BIPA exposure but all claims alleged and pre-merits; no ruling. Search snippets treated as untrusted leads and verified against named/primary-adjacent sources before logging.

2026-07-02 urgent data-quality pass: added official AISI partnership record plus first-party safety/guardrails publications; existing BIPA controversy retained and no new controversy invented.

news.title: Poland invests in ElevenLabs
news.summary: The Government of Poland took a stake in ElevenLabs via Vinci (BGK Group).
